text:
Adam Peaty wants swimming stars to be guaranteed prize money when they compete in theOlympics. In April this year, World Athletics announcedthat winning a medal in Paris would mean a financial reward, and Peaty now wants his own sport to follow suit.

Peaty, 29, has won two silver medals in France, finishing second in both the 100m breaststroke and 4x100m relay medley.

That's taken his overall Olympic medal tally to six, despitetesting positive for Covid-19during the Games.

But now Peaty has called for swimming to fall in line with other authorities. “It’s got to. I think the IOC has to give back more to the athletes now,” he toldinews.

“And so they should. We’re the entertainment. I don’t think a phone (Samsung have provided every athlete at the Games with a new phone) and the Village is enough – and the athletes are waking up to that.

“But that’s a very complex question with very complex answers, because I know the governing bodies are funding that at the moment. But World Aquatics has more than enough money to go around.”



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

To supplement his own income, the icon runs the Adam Peaty Sports Clinic as well as the AP Race Club, offering advice and guidance to swimmers. And he also cited the gruelling swimming schedule as further reason to reward competitors.

“The last 14 months have been relentless, the sacrifices I’ve had to make,” he continued, before refusing to commit to the Summer Games in Los Angeles in 2028. “It’s also not important to know that right now.

“I think the most important thing is that we relax, have a nice bottle of wine, steak, some good food at home and just do something normal. Go for a walk and enjoy that normality."

Despite not winning a gold in France, Peaty remains widely regarded as the greatest breaststroke swimmer of his era. He's the only British swimmer ever to retain an Olympic title, winning over 100m in both Rio and Tokyo.

But he's been vociferous in his criticisms of organisers in Paris. He's alsohit out at the standard of food in the Olympic village, alleging some athletes have found worms in their meals.

title: Adam Peaty demands payment for athletes after Olympians only offered free phones | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Fraser Watson
description: Adam Peaty has had some strong words for the IOC.
